headline: How Prudential’s Leadership Structure Fight Could Shape Governance Expectations for Prudential Financial (PRU) Investors
summary: On March 26, 2026, Prudential Financial filed a definitive proxy statement urging shareholders to vote against a proposal to permanently separate the Chairman and CEO roles, ahead of its May 12 annual meeting. The pushback on this governance change comes just weeks after Andrew Sullivan was appointed Chairman, underscoring how leadership structure is becoming a focal point for investors. Against this backdrop of leadership consolidation under Andrew Sullivan, we'll assess how these...

headline: Prudential Leadership Shift Puts Governance And Valuation In The Spotlight
summary: Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) has named Andrew Sullivan as Chairman of the Board, following the retirement of Charles Lowrey. A shareholder proposal seeks to require that the Chairman and CEO roles remain separate, a change the company currently opposes. These events focus attention on Prudential Financial’s leadership structure and broader corporate governance approach. Prudential Financial operates across insurance, retirement, and asset management, placing it at the intersection of...
